Class VirtualView

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am22.view.server.VirtualView
All Implemented Interfaces:
GameObserver
public class VirtualView extends Object implements GameObserver
Server-side virtual View.

As a GameObserver, every model mutation triggers a GameStateMessage broadcast to all bound ClientHandlers, regardless of the transport. The Model stays unaware of the network.

Supports batching: a single user action may produce several model notifications (setState, setActivePlayer, era/round change, ...). Inside a batch we only remember a notification arrived and emit one final GameStateMessage on close — avoids duplicate client renders.

Nickname policy. Lookup is case-insensitive and locale-stable: the map key is nickname.strip().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT), matching the policy enforced by MatchController.containsNickname. The channel keeps the nickname in its original casing for display purposes (see ClientHandler.getBoundNickname()).

      public void gameStatusChanged(Game game)

      Invoked after any change to the game state.
      Specified by:
      gameStatusChanged in interface GameObserver
      Parameters:
      game - the game whose state has just changed

    • beginBatch

      public void beginBatch()

    • endBatch

      public void endBatch()

    • endBatch

      public void endBatch(boolean broadcast)
      Closes a batch. On the outermost close, if at least one notification arrived during the batch and broadcast is true, emits a single GameStateMessage. Pass false when the wrapped call is only used to compute something (e.g. determineWinner) and the state is delivered via a dedicated message (e.g. EndGameMessage).

    • bindOrReplace

      public void bindOrReplace(String nickname, ClientHandler channel)
      Binds the nickname to the channel, replacing any previous binding (idempotent, case-insensitive).
